article_title: Meta Removes End-to-End Encryption From Instagram DMs as Take It Down Act Deadline Approaches

content_preview.title: Meta Removes End-to-End Encryption From Instagram DMs as Take It Down Act Deadline Approaches
content_preview.summary: Meta will strip end-to-end encryption from Instagram direct messages on May 8, citing low adoption, just eleven days before the Take It Down Act compels platforms to police intimate content.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Meta will discontinue end-to-end encryption for Instagram direct messages on May 8, 2026, the company confirmed in March. A Meta spokesperson told [Engadget](https://www.engadget.com/social-media/meta-is-killing-end-to-end-encryption-in-instagram-dms-195207421.html) that "very few people were opting in to end-to-end encrypted messaging in DMs, so we're removing this option from Instagram in the coming months." The decision reverses a feature introduced roughly two years ago and arri...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong privacy/policy article on Meta's Instagram DM E2EE removal.
editor_notes.source_verification: Engadget VERIFIED via WebFetch: May 8 2026 deprecation, Meta low-adoption reasoning, opt-in (not default) E2EE, WhatsApp recommendation, child safety law enforcement criticism context all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core facts verified. Take It Down Act timing connection is editorial framing not in primary source but is an established public matter.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Notes both Meta rationale and critic concerns.
